# cce-rust

> A local CLI (`cce`) that indexes a code repository — AST-chunking files with
> tree-sitter through pluggable language packs, embedding chunks, and storing a
> vector + keyword index on disk as JSON — and answers queries with hybrid vector
> + BM25 retrieval. It is a test-first Rust implementation of SPEC v1.0, a
> Dashboard addendum (v1.1), the v2.0 language-packs evolution (SPEC-V2), v2.1
> secret protection (SPEC-V2.1), v2.2 workspace mode (SPEC-V2.2 — federating a
> multi-codebase ecosystem while each member stays isolated), v2.3 CCE Sync
> (SPEC-SYNC — an offline-first, content-addressed git cache for the index, with a
> byte-exact cross-language artifact), and v2.4 CCE MCP (SPEC-MCP — a Model Context
> Protocol server so an agent uses CCE as a native `context_search` tool, plus
> `cce init` to wire an editor up plug-and-play), the v2.4.1 consolidation (a
> refreshed dashboard — agent-vs-human, per-package, index-freshness, and
> secret-safety panels — plus a verified, offline-first documentation sweep), and
> the v2.5 Savings Layers (SPEC-V2.5-SAVINGS — compact-by-default AST chunk
> compression with expand-on-demand, output/grammar compression, local secret-safe
> memory, deterministic turn summarization, a seven-bucket `cce savings` ledger and
> `cce eval` A/B harness, exposed as nine cross-language MCP tools; savings are
> reported honestly, vs a full-file baseline, not the real end-to-end agent cost),
> and v2.6 Knowledge Sources (a markdown-heading chunker, the neutral
> `cce.knowledge/v1` NDJSON ingest contract behind `cce knowledge index`, and a
> `source: code|knowledge|both` retrieval blend with provenance and staleness
> weighting; the indexer also honors committed `.gitignore` for
> builder-independent artifacts), and the CCE Sync consumer mode (repo-less:
> `cce sync list` enumerates a cache, `cce sync pull --all` synthesizes a
> ready-to-search workspace with zero source checkouts, the cache self-describes
> by publishing workspace metadata, and `cce sync verify --checksum-only` is a
> source-free integrity check), and M5 knowledge-corpus sync (SPEC-SYNC-KNOWLEDGE
> — `cce knowledge push`/`pull` move the built, redacted corpus through the same
> git cache under an additive `knowledge/…` key space; corpora surface in
> `cce sync list`, install via `pull --all`, verify with `--checksum-only`, and
> report freshness in MCP `index_status`; trust is stated honestly —
> not rebuild-verifiable by consumers, trust-the-pusher + the git ACL); six packs ship
> (Python, JavaScript, Ruby, Rust, TypeScript, C) and every chunk records the
> exact tree-sitter node type in a `kind` field. A sibling Ruby
> implementation was built from the identical specs. MIT licensed, © 2026 David
> Silva. No network calls by default: the semantic embedder over localhost is
> opt-in, `cce dashboard` is a loopback-only, read-only, self-contained UI, and
> `cce mcp` is a read-only, stdio-only MCP server.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): What it is, install/setup for macOS and Ubuntu, worked usage examples, tests/coverage.
- [SPEC.md](SPEC.md): The normative base-engine specification (v1.0).
- [DASHBOARD-SPEC.md](DASHBOARD-SPEC.md): The dashboard & observability addendum (v1.1) — metrics log, aggregator, dashboard.
- [SPEC-V2.md](SPEC-V2.md): The language-packs evolution spec (v2.0) — pack interface, registry, validators, `kind`, conformance shape.
- [SPEC-V2.1.md](SPEC-V2.1.md): The secret-protection evolution spec (v2.1) — sensitive-file skip + secret redaction.
- [SPEC-V2.2.md](SPEC-V2.2.md): The workspace-mode evolution spec (v2.2) — detection, manifest, federation, cross-member edges.
- [SPEC-SYNC.md](SPEC-SYNC.md): The CCE Sync design spec (v2.3) — content-addressed cache, artifact format, remotes, offline-first.
- [SPEC-SYNC-KNOWLEDGE.md](SPEC-SYNC-KNOWLEDGE.md): The knowledge-corpus sync spec (M5) — the `.cck` container, the `knowledge/…` key space, the six decisions (identity/trust/access/freshness/retention/redaction), the consumer surface, and the CI ingestion reference.
- [SPEC-MCP.md](SPEC-MCP.md): The CCE MCP design spec (v2.4) — the stdio MCP server, the first three tools, `cce init`, sync freshness.
- [SPEC-V2.5-SAVINGS.md](SPEC-V2.5-SAVINGS.md): The Savings Layers spec (v2.5) — the seven layers, the token estimator, the ledger, and the six new MCP tools.
- [Workspace](docs/workspace.md): Workspace model, `workspace.yml`, detection rules, federation semantics, where it strains.
- [Sync](docs/sync.md): CCE Sync model, artifact format, content address, consumer mode (repo-less list / pull --all / checksum-only verify), permissions, troubleshooting.
- [MCP](docs/mcp.md): CCE MCP — the server, the **nine tools**, `cce init`, sync freshness, and how to confirm agent use.
- [Savings](docs/savings.md): The seven Savings Layers, the `cce.tokens/v1` estimator, `cce savings`, the `cce eval` A/B harness, and the honest "vs full-file baseline" framing.
- [Knowledge](docs/knowledge.md): Knowledge Sources (v2.6) — the markdown-heading chunker, the `cce.knowledge/v1` contract, `cce knowledge index`, the `source:` retrieval blend with provenance + staleness weighting, and corpus sync (`cce knowledge push`/`pull`, the consumer flow, freshness, trust).
- [Verified](docs/VERIFIED.md): Offline + online cold-start verification transcripts (index/search/stats/dashboard/workspace/MCP offline; CCE Sync online).
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md): Newcomer path from install to first successful index + search.
- [How it works](docs/how-it-works.md): The benefit and the mechanism, in diagrams — why serving chunks beats reading whole files.
- [Adding a language](docs/adding-a-language.md): Step-by-step guide to adding a language pack, with a worked example.
- [How-to recipes](docs/how-to.md): Task recipes for index, search, packs, feedback, dashboard, stats, sync (incl. consuming a team cache repo-less), bench, conformance, and Ollama.
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): Design goals, module map, pipeline, the language-pack architecture, and where the design strains.
- [Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md): The metrics pipeline (log → aggregator → API → page), event schema, and aggregation formulas.
- [Decisions](docs/DECISIONS.md): How each ambiguity in the spec was resolved, with rationale.
- [TDD log](docs/TDD.md): The red → green build order and coverage (605 tests, ~94% line coverage).
- [Benchmarks](docs/BENCHMARKS.md): Measured index/latency/recall numbers per language (Ruby/Rust/TypeScript/C) on real corpora.

## Source (key files)

- [src/lib.rs](src/lib.rs): Crate root; module tree and public surface.
- [src/main.rs](src/main.rs): CLI (clap) — index, search, packs, feedback, dashboard, stats, bench, conformance, workspace, sync, mcp, init, savings, knowledge, eval.
- [src/config.rs](src/config.rs): Normative constants (SPEC §3, DASHBOARD-SPEC §1) and runtime config, incl. the v2.5 retrieval/output/memory/summarization/savings config and the v2.6 markdown/knowledge config.
- [src/tokenizer.rs](src/tokenizer.rs): The shared byte-exact tokenizer (SPEC §4.1) and the `cce.tokens/v1` savings estimator, `max(1, floor(bytes/4))` (SPEC-V2.5 §4).
- [src/chunker.rs](src/chunker.rs): Generic tree-sitter chunking, chunk IDs, the `kind` field (SPEC §4.2–4.4, SPEC-V2 §3–4) — references no language.
- [src/packs/mod.rs](src/packs/mod.rs): The `LanguagePack` trait and the pack `Registry` (SPEC-V2 §1).
- [src/packs/](src/packs/): One pack per language (python, javascript, ruby, rust, typescript, c) and the three-layer `validators` (SPEC-V2 §2, §5).
- [src/embedder.rs](src/embedder.rs): Hashing embedder, cosine, rounding, optional Ollama (SPEC §5, §11).
- [src/retriever.rs](src/retriever.rs): The hybrid retrieval pipeline (SPEC §6).
- [src/store.rs](src/store.rs): Index assembly, JSON persistence, whole-file token counts (SPEC §7, DASHBOARD-SPEC §3).
- [src/conformance.rs](src/conformance.rs): The byte-stable conformance.json emitter (SPEC §8).
- [src/metrics.rs](src/metrics.rs): The persisted metrics event log — injected clock/id source (the one place wall-clock time is allowed), best-effort append, robust reader (DASHBOARD-SPEC §2).
- [src/aggregator.rs](src/aggregator.rs): The pure aggregator — totals, north-stars, daily series, deltas, plus the v2.4.1 agent-vs-human, secret-safety, and index-freshness sections (DASHBOARD-SPEC §4).
- [src/dashboard.rs](src/dashboard.rs): The loopback-only, read-only, self-contained dashboard web server; serves the refreshed v2.4.1 panels, all purely log-derived so it makes zero network calls (DASHBOARD-SPEC §6).
- [src/federation.rs](src/federation.rs): Federated indexing/search/stats/dashboard over workspace members (SPEC-V2.2 §4–7).
- [src/sync/](src/sync/): CCE Sync — identity helpers, byte-exact artifacts (`.cce` code + `.cck` knowledge), `sync.*`/`knowledge.sync.*` config, git wrappers, the `SyncRemote` git backend, `init/push/pull/list/status/verify` including the repo-less consumer mode (`list`, `pull --all`, `verify --checksum-only`), and `knowledge push/pull` (SPEC-SYNC, SPEC-SYNC-KNOWLEDGE).
- [src/mcp/](src/mcp/): CCE MCP — JSON-RPC 2.0 framing (`protocol`), the stdio dispatch loop + per-session ledger (`server`), the **nine tools** (`tools`), and `cce init` (`init`) (SPEC-MCP, SPEC-V2.5 §6).
- [src/compress.rs](src/compress.rs), [src/grammar.rs](src/grammar.rs): L2 AST chunk compression (`signature`/`compact`/`full`) and the L3 byte-pinned result grammar (SPEC-V2.5 §2).
- [src/memory.rs](src/memory.rs), [src/session.rs](src/session.rs): L5 local secret-scrubbed decision memory + precision recall, and the L6 deterministic per-session digest (SPEC-V2.5 §2).
- [src/savings.rs](src/savings.rs), [src/pricing.rs](src/pricing.rs), [src/eval.rs](src/eval.rs): the seven-bucket ledger + honesty note, the embedded offline pricing table, and the correctness-gated cost-primary A/B eval aggregator (SPEC-V2.5 §3/§7).
- [src/markdown.rs](src/markdown.rs), [src/knowledge/](src/knowledge/): the v2.6 Knowledge Sources — the markdown-heading chunker, the `cce.knowledge/v1` contract, the snapshot-keyed store, and the retrieval blend with provenance + staleness rules (SPEC-V2.6 §2–5).
- [src/walker.rs](src/walker.rs): the gitignore-aware walk — committed `.gitignore` only, machine-local excludes deliberately not honored, `.git/`/`.cce/` always pruned (SPEC §7.1, v2.6.3).

## Project

- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): Dev setup, the three quality gates, TDD discipline, conformance rules.
- [Security](SECURITY.md): Threat model (local filesystem trust boundary, no network by default) and private reporting.
- [Governance](GOVERNANCE.md): Single-maintainer/BDFL model with the spec as constitution.
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): Keep a Changelog / SemVer history.
- [Releasing](RELEASING.md): The automated, tag-driven release process behind the prebuilt binaries.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Instructions for AI agents working in this repo.

## Optional

- [Ruby sibling](https://github.com/davidslv/cce-ruby): The parallel clean-room implementation from the same spec.
- [Blog: "The spec was the program"](https://davidslv.uk/2026/07/05/the-spec-was-the-program.html): The write-up of the experiment.
